data.title: NABARD accepts bids for over 3-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) has accepted bids worth 44.12 billion rupees ($513.75 million) for bonds maturing in three years, two months and 11 days, three bankers said on Friday.

The state-owned company will pay an annual coupon of 7.53% on this issue and had invited coupon and commitment bids from bankers and investors earlier in the day, they said.

The company did not immediately reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

Here is the list of deals reported so far on Jan. 10:

data.title: A-Sonic Aerospace Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
A-Sonic Aerospace bought back 30,000 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$9,348, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The aerospace equipment and logistics company has been authorized to buy back around 10.7 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 418,600 shares.

data.title: Swedish Construction Output Rises for First Time in Over 2 Years
data.description:
Construction activity in Sweden rose by 1.0% year-on-year in November 2024, reversing a downwardly revised 1.5% fall in the previous month and marking the first increase since August 2022.

The construction sector accounts for 9.2% of the total value added in the private sector.

data.title: Godrej Properties Acquires 24-Acre Land in Indore, India for Residential Development
data.description:
Godrej Properties has acquired a 24-acre land parcel in Indore, India for an undisclosed amount to develop residential units, according to a Friday filing to the stock exchanges.

The land parcel is estimated to have a saleable area of 620,000 square feet with a revenue potential of 5 billion rupees.

This is the real estate developer's second land acquisition in Indore following the purchase of 46 acres of land in July 2024.

Shares of the company were down over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Swedish Monthly GDP Rebounds in November
data.description:
The Swedish economy expanded 1.4% month-over-month in November 2024, recovering from a 0.4% contraction in October. “November saw an upturn in economic activity with growth in both goods- and service-producing industries as well as in general government production.

ICT services showed the largest growth figures among services while stronger manufacturing and a continued recovery in construction both contributed to the upturn for goods-producers”, says Mattias Kain Wyatt, an economist at Statistics Sweden.

data.title: Gold Bulls Growing Stronger, But Bearish Bias Lingers, Chart Shows — Market Talk
data.description: Gold bulls are growing stronger after Comex gold futures closed above the 50-day simple moving average line on Thursday, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. Also, the relative strength index indicator remains above the 50% threshold, signaling gold price's positive momentum is re-accelerating, the analyst says. However, the technical setup is still bearish, given resistance at the $2,700/oz level is holding firm, Chai says. Immediate support is pegged at $2,600/oz, the analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.3% higher at $2,676.44/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Sainsbury's To Increase Salaries of Hourly-Paid Workers by 5% in the Year Ahead
Airbus Plane Deliveries Expected to Pick Up in 2Q — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus missed its plane delivery target for 2024 but the European plane maker's deliveries should start to pick up in the second quarter of the year, Citi analysts Charles J Armitage and Sam Burgess say in a research note. Airbus said Thursday that it had delivered 766 commercial aircraft to customers in 2024, beating the previous year's figure, but short of the 770 it had guided for. "We believe 1Q 2025 deliveries are likely to be weak, due to few aircraft in inventory following the strong December," the analysts say. They expect deliveries to pick up in 2Q as new LEAP engine blades are built and put into new engines. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: Sainsbury's Sees FY25 Retail Adj Oper Pft in Line With Market Views
European Markets Poised for Muted Open
data.description:
European equity markets were set to open little changed on Friday as caution dominated sentiment ahead of a crucial US jobs report that could cement bets on fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year.

In Europe, investors will be watching for November data on French industrial production and Italian retail sales.

Meanwhile, there are no major earnings reports in Europe scheduled for Friday.

In premarket trading, Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures remained flat to slightly negative.

data.title: Qilu Expressway Forms Joint Venture to Invest in Wind Power Tower Project
data.description:
Qilu Expressway agreed to form a joint venture company with Shandong Zhengchen Technology, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The company plans to invest in a wind power hybrid tower industrial base project with the fixed station planned to be set up at the Pingyin Smart Transportation Industrial Base of its subsidiary, Qilu Expressway (Shandong) Assembly, in China.

The company will be investing 51 million yuan in the capital of the JV company and will have a 51% shareholding in it. The remaining 49% stake will be held by Shandong Zhengchen Technology.

The shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Liaoning SG Automotive Logs 197% Surge in Vehicle Sales in December 2024
data.description:
Liaoning SG Automotive Group said its total vehicle sales surged 197% to 677 units in December 2024 from 228 units a year earlier.

Total vehicle production went up 84% year over year to 430 units from 234 units, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the vehicle and component manufacturer were down 1% in recent trade.

Romania Q3 GDP Growth Revised Higher
data.description:
Romania's GDP expanded by 1.2% year-on-year in the third quarter of 2024, faster than the second estimate of 1.1% and accelerating from a 0.9% rise in the previous three-month period.

This marked the fourteenth consecutive quarter of economic expansion and the fastest pace since Q4 of 2023.

On a seasonally adjusted quarterly basis, the economy showed no growth in the September quarter, after a 0.1% expansion in the previous quarter.
